title: Podman option to hide header
body:
/kind feature

Can we get an option to silence the header information please?  I hate having to always tack on `| egrep -v "REPOSITORY|CONTAINER|etc"` in scripts

```
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES
edb291d0a4b4  localhost/kubevirt-userguide:latest  /bin/bash -c mkdo...  7 seconds ago  Up 7 seconds ago  0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp  userguide
```


This would be awesome....
```
$ podman ps -q
edb291d0a4b4  localhost/kubevirt-userguide:latest  /bin/bash -c mkdo...  7 seconds ago  Up 7 seconds ago  0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp  userguide
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Fri Feb 19 08:56:17 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
You can use the --format flag. 
```
podman ps --format "{{.ID}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Command}}\t{{.CreatedHuman}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Ports}}\t{{.Names}}"
```
I think this allows the most flexible use cases for scripting as you can easily add and omit fields.

I am fine with adding --noheading to all of the commands that print a heading.  --quiet has special meaning, and so I am not crazy about adding it for this use case.  As @Luap99 you can do this with --format, but that seems to be a fairly advanced scripting requirement.

@mazzystr interested in opening a PR?

@Luap99  Ug, I'd rather just add a pipe grep.  :/

@rhatdan Trade ya a PR for an issue [issue](https://github.com/kubevirt/community/issues/95) ;)
